instruction: Add concise docstrings to every public function in src/main.py describing what it does, its parameters, and its return value.
Add a TypeError check if n is not an integer and a test for it.
Add a function fib_generator(n: int) that yields Fibonacci numbers one at a time. Add tests.
Refactor fibonacci() to use a helper function _fib_pair(a, b, count) that builds the sequence recursively. Keep behavior identical.
Change fibonacci() to return a tuple instead of a list, and update the tests to match.
Add docstrings to all methods in the Stack class.
Add a check that push rejects None values with a ValueError and add a test for it.
Add a clear() method to Stack that removes all items, and add a test for it.
Refactor Stack to use collections.deque internally instead of a list. Keep all public behavior identical.
Add a max_size parameter to __init__ that raises ValueError if push would exceed it. Update tests.
Add a docstring to merge_sorted describing the merge algorithm and its O(n+m) complexity.
Add input validation that raises TypeError if either argument is not a list, and add a test.
Add a merge_k_sorted(lists: list[list[int]]) function that merges k sorted lists. Add tests.
Refactor merge_sorted to use heapq.merge instead of manual pointer logic. Keep behavior identical.
Change merge_sorted to merge in descending order (largest first) and update tests.
Add a module-level docstring and docstrings to all functions in src/main.py.
Add a check that raises ValueError if the expression contains invalid characters (anything other than digits, operators, parentheses, dots, whitespace). Add a test.
Add support for the modulo operator (%) with the same precedence as multiplication. Add tests.
Refactor calculate() to use a tokenizer + shunting-yard algorithm instead of the current approach. Keep all tests passing.
Change calculate() to return an int when the result has no fractional part, and a float otherwise. Update tests.
Add a docstring to word_frequency explaining case-insensitivity, punctuation stripping, and the empty-string return.
Add a TypeError check if text is not a string, and add a test.
Add a top_n_words(text: str, n: int) function that returns the n most frequent words as a list of (word, count) tuples. Add tests.
Refactor word_frequency to use collections.Counter instead of a manual dict. Keep behavior identical.
Change word_frequency to use str.casefold() instead of str.lower() and add a test for the Turkish dotless i.
Add a docstring to parse_csv explaining the header row, type inference, and ValueError conditions.
Add a check that raises ValueError if the input is not a string and add a test.
Add support for quoted CSV fields containing commas. Add tests with quoted fields.
Refactor parse_csv to use the csv module instead of manual splitting. Keep all tests passing.
Change parse_csv to return all values as strings (no type inference) and update the tests.
Add docstrings to all methods in the Graph class.
Add a TypeError check if add_edge receives non-hashable node values, and add a test.
Add a count_edges() method to Graph that returns the total number of edges, and add a test.
Refactor Graph to use collections.defaultdict for adjacency lists instead of manual dict checks. Keep behavior identical.
Rename add_edge to connect throughout src/main.py and tests/test_main.py, preserving all behavior.
Add a module-level docstring and a docstring to evaluate() explaining supported operators and precedence.
Add a ValueError with a descriptive message if the expression is empty or whitespace-only, and add a test.
Add support for the modulo operator (%) with the same precedence as * and /. Add tests.
Refactor evaluate() to use a Pratt parser instead of recursive descent. Keep all tests passing.
Replace '**' exponentiation with '^' having the same precedence and right-associativity. Update tests.
Add docstrings to the TokenBucket class and all its methods.
Add a TypeError check if consume receives a non-integer tokens value, and add a test.
Add a __repr__ method to TokenBucket showing capacity, refill_rate, and current tokens. Add a test.
Refactor TokenBucket to use a _refill() helper method that updates tokens before every operation. Keep behavior identical.
Change consume() to accept a float tokens parameter instead of int, and update tests.
Add docstrings to the MinHeap class and all its methods.
Add a check that peek() and pop() raise IndexError with a descriptive message, and add tests verifying the message content.
Add an __iter__ method to MinHeap that yields items in non-decreasing order without modifying the heap, and add tests.
Refactor MinHeap to use heapq internally instead of manual sift-up/sift-down. Keep all public behavior identical.
Change MinHeap to a MaxHeap (return the largest item on pop/peek) and update tests.
Add docstrings to BoundedQueue and all its methods.
Add a ValueError if put is called with maxsize=0 queue, and add a test.
Add optional timeout parameters to put(item, timeout=None) and get(timeout=None) that raise queue.Full or queue.Empty on timeout. Add tests.
Refactor BoundedQueue to use queue.Queue from the stdlib internally, keeping the same public interface and tests passing.
Change BoundedQueue so get() returns items in LIFO order instead of FIFO. Update tests.
Add docstrings to rle_encode and rle_decode explaining the encoding format and ValueError conditions.
Add a TypeError check if the input is not a string, and add a test.
Add a rle_compress(data: str) -> bytes function that returns the RLE encoding as packed bytes. Add tests.
Refactor rle_encode to use itertools.groupby instead of manual run-length tracking. Keep behavior identical.
Change the encoding format so a single character is NOT followed by '1' (i.e. 'a' stays 'a', not 'a1'). Update encode and decode and tests.
Add docstrings to is_palindrome and make_palindrome.
Add a TypeError check if the input is not a string, and add a test.
Add a function all_palindromes(s: str) -> list[str] that returns all palindromic substrings of s. Add tests.
Rewrite make_palindrome to use a recursive helper instead of the current algorithm, keeping the same results.
Change make_palindrome to return the longest palindrome that contains the input as a prefix (not necessarily the shortest). Update tests.
Add docstrings to encode_cyclic and decode_cyclic.
Add a TypeError check if the input is not a string, and add a test.
Add a rotate_string(s: str, n: int) function that rotates s by n positions. Add tests.
Refactor encode_cyclic to use a list comprehension and str.join instead of a for loop. Keep behavior identical.
Generalize encode_cyclic and decode_cyclic to accept a group_size parameter (default 3). Update and add tests.
Add a docstring to triples_sum_to_zero explaining the algorithm and complexity.
Add a TypeError check if numbers is not a list, and add a test.
Add a function pairs_sum_to_zero(numbers: list[int]) -> bool that checks for two distinct elements summing to zero. Add tests.
Refactor triples_sum_to_zero to use itertools.combinations instead of nested loops. Keep behavior identical.
Change triples_sum_to_zero to return the sorted tuple of three indices whose values sum to zero, or None. Update tests.
Add a docstring to smallest_change explaining the algorithm.
Add a TypeError check if numbers is not a list, and add a test.
Add a function is_palindrome_list(numbers: list[int]) -> bool that checks if a list is palindromic. Add tests.
Rewrite smallest_change using dynamic programming with memoization. Keep the same signature and results.
Change smallest_change to also return the indices that need changing as a list, not just the count. Update tests.
Add a docstring to closest_integer explaining the rounding rule and tie-breaking behavior.
Add a ValueError check if the input string is not a valid number, and add a test.
Add a function round_list(values: list[str]) -> list[int] that applies closest_integer to each. Add tests.
Refactor closest_integer to use the decimal module for parsing and rounding instead of the current approach. Keep behavior identical.
Change the tie-breaking rule to round towards zero instead of away from zero, and update tests.
Add a docstring to get_row explaining the sorting rules.
Add a TypeError check if grid is not a list of lists, and add a test.
Add a function get_spiral_order(grid: list[list[int]]) -> list[int] that returns elements in clockwise spiral order. Add tests.
Refactor get_row to use list comprehensions instead of explicit for loops. Keep behavior identical.
Change get_row to return columns in ascending order instead of descending, and update tests.
Add a docstring to sort_array explaining the odd/even sum rule.
Add a TypeError check if numbers is not a list, and add a test.
Add a function sort_array_desc(numbers: list[int]) -> list[int] that always sorts descending. Add tests.
Refactor sort_array to use a single sorted() call with a key function. Keep behavior identical.
Change sort_array to sort the input list in place and return None. Update tests to call with a copy.
Add a docstring to largest_prime_digit_sum and any internal helpers.
Add a TypeError check if numbers is not a list, and add a test.
Add a function top_n_prime_digit_sums(numbers: list[int], n: int) -> list[int] that returns the top n digit sums of primes in descending order. Add tests.
Refactor _is_prime to use the Sieve of Eratosthenes for batch checking. Keep behavior identical.
Change largest_prime_digit_sum to return the largest prime itself instead of its digit sum. Update tests.
